<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed missing Prisma migrations causing 500 errors on Render deployment</title>
  <subtitle>Created initial database migration files to enable schema deployment on Render's PostgreSQL instance.</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Homepage returned 500 errors while /api/health returned 200 because DailyMessage table queries failed</fact>
    <fact>prisma/migrations directory was completely missing from the repository</fact>
    <fact>Created prisma/migrations/20260405000000_init/migration.sql with CREATE statements for User, Vessel, Booking, PasswordReset, and DailyMessage tables</fact>
    <fact>Created prisma/migrations/migration_lock.toml to lock PostgreSQL as the database provider</fact>
    <fact>Migration includes all enums (Role, BookingType, BookingStatus), indexes, and foreign key constraints</fact>
    <fact>Committed migration files with commit 5cc3ce6 and pushed to trigger new Render deployment</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The Render deployment succeeded for the build process but failed at runtime when the Next.js application attempted to query database tables that didn't exist. The API health endpoint worked because it doesn't require database access, but the homepage crashed trying to fetch DailyMessage data. The root cause was the absence of any Prisma migration files in the repository, which meant the `prisma migrate deploy` command in Render's build script had nothing to apply to the production PostgreSQL database. The fix involved creating a complete initial migration file containing the entire database schema (all 5 tables with their relationships, enums, indexes, and constraints) along with the migration lock file. This enables Prisma to properly initialize the database schema on the next deployment.</narrative>

Application successfully deployed to https://westcoast-marine-parks.onrender.com with all endpoints returning 200 OK.</completed>

  <notes>The debugging process revealed a common pattern in serverless/PaaS deployments: dependencies categorization matters significantly. Tools needed at build time must be in dependencies (not devDependencies) when NODE_ENV=production. Database schema initialization through migrations is a critical first-deploy step that must be committed to the repository beforehand.</notes>
</summary>
